title: Tries, Prefix Search, and String Algorithms · Counterexample Repair Bay
summary: Build an autocomplete and route-prefix service with predictable matching behavior. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Unicode-aware prefix index and matcher and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent shared prefixes, terminal markers, and child transitions without confusing words and prefixes.
Implement insert, exact lookup, prefix lookup, delete, and bounded suggestions.
Define Unicode normalization and case policy before indexing or comparison.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
